welcome.title: Welcome to Yearly Planning
welcome.description: This guided process will help you set a clear direction for the year ahead.

processSteps.step2: Review your values and year theme
processSteps.step3: Write your story & dream about your fantastic day
processSteps.step4: Run a dreaming exercise to prime your prediction engine
processSteps.step6: Write baseline narratives for each area
processSteps.step7: Define priorities with success signals and constraints

values.title: Values Check-in
values.description: Take a moment to review and reflect on your values. These shape the priorities you set next.
values.noLifeAreas: No life areas defined yet. Add some in the Life Areas section.

yearTheme.title: Theme of the Year
yearTheme.description: A single word or short phrase that captures the spirit of your year.
yearTheme.examples: Examples: Growth, Balance, Adventure, Simplify, Create, Connect, Focus, Joy

story.title: Your Story
story.description: Write a brief narrative about how you want this year to unfold.
story.placeholder: What do you want the story of this year to be?

fantasticDay.title: Your Fantastic Day
fantasticDay.description: Describe your ideal day in this coming year in vivid detail. What do you see, hear, feel?

lifeWheel.title: Life Wheel Check-in
lifeWheel.description: Rate your satisfaction with each life area to identify where to focus.
lifeWheel.noLifeAreas: You have no life areas defined. Create some in the Life Areas section to use the Life Wheel.

focusAreas.title: Select Your Focus Life Areas
focusAreas.description: Choose the areas you want to dedicate the most energy to this year.

baselines.title: Life Area Baseline Narratives
baselines.description: For each focus area, describe where you are now. This creates a starting point to measure progress against.
baselines.placeholder: Where are you now in this area? What's working? What needs attention?

prioritiesStep.title: Set Your Priorities
prioritiesStep.description: Define the priorities that will guide your actions this year.
prioritiesStep.emptyDescription: Add your first priority to get started.

confirmStep.saveError: Failed to save your yearly plan

batteryCheckIn.title: Battery Check-In
batteryCheckIn.subtitle: Rate each battery on a 1-5 scale.
batteryCheckIn.definitionDemand: Demand = how much load this battery carried during the week.
batteryCheckIn.definitionState: State = how charged this battery feels at the end of the week.
batteryCheckIn.quickNotePlaceholder: What most impacted this battery score?
batteryCheckIn.validationError: Please rate demand and state for all four batteries before completing reflection.

processSteps.step7: Let go of what no longer serves you

dreamingExercise.title: Dreaming
dreamingExercise.subtitle: Prime your prediction engine

intro.paragraph1: Before you begin creating goals, dream. Every goal is attached to a dream: without an associated dream, a goal has no emotional power, and emotions create habits. The more detailed the dream, the more your brain will believe it's already happened.
intro.paragraph2: Your brain is a prediction engine. Dreaming makes it more likely you'll achieve the outcomes you want from your new year planning process by priming that prediction engine.
intro.quote: Prediction is such a fundamental activity of the human brain that some scientists consider it the brain's primary mode of operation. Everything you feel is based on prediction from your knowledge and experience. You are truly an architect of your experience. Believing is feeling.
intro.quoteAttribution: Lisa Feldman Barrett, How Emotions are Made
intro.paragraph3: As the architect of your experience, answer the following questions in as much detail as you can. Several questions ask you to look at your outcomes through the eyes of the most important people in your life, your "VIPs." This helps you get out of your own negativity bias, seeing your progress through their perspective. These questions also highlight the strengths and skills you already have that you can lean on for resilience when life gets challenging.

dreamingExercise.listSubtitle: Add as many as you can
dreamingExercise.textSubtitle: Write freely -- there are no wrong answers

outcomes.question: Suppose the next year went exactly the way that was right for you: At the end of the year, what would you notice you had done? What else? What else? Add as many details as possible.
outcomes.example: Completed a professional certification. Ran a half-marathon. Read 24 books. Took a family vacation abroad.

difference.question: What difference will doing those things make?
difference.example: Taking a skills improvement course each morning would allow me to move up in my career and make more money so I can buy a bigger house for my growing family where we can share happy memories together.
difference.placeholder: Describe the difference it would make...

worthDoing.question: What would tell you that doing what you did had been worth doing?
worthDoing.example: My children work hard on their education because they see my example.
worthDoing.placeholder: What would tell you it was worth it...

vipsNotice.question: What would VIPs notice that would tell them what you had done had been the right thing for you?
vipsNotice.example: I would have a smile on my face each time they saw me.
vipsNotice.placeholder: What would the important people in your life notice...

vipAfterYear.question: If a VIP hadn't seen you for the year and then saw you, what would they notice about you that would tell them you were living your preferred future?
vipAfterYear.example: I was smiling and giving them a big hug full of love. They could see my excitement as I told them about the things I'd learned.
vipAfterYear.placeholder: What would they notice about you...

vipsSeeInYou.question: What do your VIPs see in you that tells them you can make the best of next year?
vipsSeeInYou.example: They saw I had time for my learning, my work, my family and my hobbies since I had scheduled my calendar with intention during a regular weekly review process.
vipsSeeInYou.placeholder: What strengths do they see in you...

vipsNoticeProgress.question: What would your VIPs notice that tells them you're making progress?
vipsNoticeProgress.example: I celebrated with them each section of the course that I completed.
vipsNoticeProgress.placeholder: What signs of progress would they see...

knowAboutYourself.question: What do you know about yourself now that tells you have what it takes to make this next year best for you?
knowAboutYourself.example: I know I've been through hard times before and had the resilience to keep going. I keep a list of the strengths I used in the past, even the smallest ones.
knowAboutYourself.placeholder: What do you already know about your strengths...

oneClue.question: What's one clue, even the smallest, that tells you you're on the right path to make the coming year just right for you?
oneClue.example: I completed some or all of this workbook.
oneClue.placeholder: One small clue you're on the right path...

progressClues.question: What are clues you'd notice that would tell you you're making progress? These clues are the seeds for creating your goals.
progressClues.example: I created a note with several skills courses that I can take that will help in my career direction. I signed up for a course. I completed the first module.
progressClues.placeholder: A clue that tells you you're making progress...
